title: Add Falcon Cloaking Mode Options (about:blank, https://error, 123456789101112)

requirements.text: Add a cloaking mode selector to the Falcon Cloaking feature in the frontend. The user must be able to choose from three disguise modes before or when triggering the cloak: (1) 'about:blank' — opens the app in a popup/tab that shows about:blank in the address bar; (2) 'https://error' — opens the app disguised under an https://error URL appearance; (3) '123456789101112' — opens the app disguised under a numeric string URL appearance. Persist the selected mode in component state so it is remembered across cloak triggers within the session.

quotes: make a options for the cloaking the cloaking is for the falcon only and you can choose for the falcon 1, about:blank 2, https://error 3, 123456789101112

acceptanceCriteria: A mode selector (e.g., dropdown or segmented control) is visible in the Falcon Cloaking section of the UI, presenting options: 'about:blank', 'https://error', and '123456789101112'.
Selecting 'about:blank' and triggering the cloak opens the app in a new blank-URL popup/tab with about:blank visible in the browser address bar.
Selecting 'https://error' and triggering the cloak opens the app in a new popup/tab where the address bar shows https://error.
Selecting '123456789101112' and triggering the cloak opens the app in a new popup/tab where the address bar shows the numeric string '123456789101112'.
The selected mode persists in state and is pre-selected the next time the user opens the cloak options.
The mode selector and cloak trigger are only present in the Falcon Cloaking UI — not in the main browser toolbar or tab bar.

requirements.text: Update useCloaking.ts to accept a cloakMode parameter ('about:blank' | 'https://error' | '123456789101112') and apply the appropriate URL disguise technique for each mode. For 'about:blank', use the existing blank popup approach. For 'https://error', open the popup/window and push or replace the history state to display 'https://error' as the visible URL. For '123456789101112', open the popup/window and set the visible URL to the numeric string '123456789101112' using the History API or equivalent popup technique.

quotes: makethis cloak for the url too

acceptanceCriteria: useCloaking.ts exports a cloak function that accepts a mode argument.
When mode is 'about:blank', the cloaked window/tab shows about:blank in the browser address bar.
When mode is 'https://error', the cloaked window/tab shows https://error in the browser address bar.
When mode is '123456789101112', the cloaked window/tab shows 123456789101112 in the browser address bar.
In all three modes, the full Cheetah app content renders inside the cloaked window.

requirements.text: Update CloakButton.tsx to integrate the new mode selector UI. Replace or augment the existing single-action cloak button with a compact control that shows the current selected mode and allows switching between the three modes ('about:blank', 'https://error', '123456789101112'). The cloak action button should trigger the cloak using the currently selected mode. Style the selector consistently with the existing dark cheetah theme (dark background, yellow-orange accents).

quotes: make a options for the cloaking the cloaking is for the falcon only

acceptanceCriteria: CloakButton.tsx renders a mode selector alongside the falcon cloak trigger button.
The mode selector displays the three options: 'about:blank', 'https://error', '123456789101112'.
Clicking the cloak button triggers the selected mode via the updated useCloaking hook.
The component is styled using the existing dark/yellow-orange cheetah color tokens from Tailwind config.
No other toolbar components are modified.

constraints: Cloaking options and mode selector must only appear in the Falcon Cloaking UI — do not expose them elsewhere in the app.
Do not modify files under frontend/src/hooks/useInternetIdentity.ts, frontend/src/hooks/useActor.ts, frontend/src/main.tsx, or frontend/src/components/ui.
No backend changes are required for this feature.
nonGoals: Adding any new cloaking modes beyond the three specified (about:blank, https://error, 123456789101112).
Persisting the selected cloak mode to the backend or across browser sessions.
Modifying the proxy, search, or tab system logic.
Changing the existing hotkey (Cmd+Tab+H) behavior unrelated to mode selection.
